pipeline_phases_complete: Phase 3.5 (gap pursuit): G1 (codebase, 9 claims) + G2 (web, 9 claims)
refuted_corrections: C-013/C-047: 6 JSONL files corrected to 4 (.json files are not .jsonl)
PR #10 R2 substitution count: 782 corrected to 776 (git stat verified)
gap_pursuit_additions: C-G001-C-G002: Tenet identification is filename prefix only (type:tenet unused)
C-G003-C-G005/C-G009: Project-directory names are reversible path-encodings not hashes
C-G006-C-G008: Canonical MCP config is ~/.claude.json not ~/.claude/mcp.json
C-G010-C-G014: No dotfiles manager has native ephemeral-host support; CI=true guard is cheapest v1
C-G015-C-G018: No ecosystem has clean per-key JSON portability; sidecar file pattern is cheapest v1
key_cross_cutting_findings: always-last-step rule: state records are the final write in every movement operation across all four companions
drift-record-style pattern is the JASON-OS house style for lightweight state (4 existing .claude/state/*.jsonl files confirm this)
schema versioning mirrors across all four data structures -- planner can write one shared schema-versioning policy
fast-path is a mode of the dashboard, not a bypass -- fast-path hits surface before execution across all four companions
security pattern battery (sanitize-error.cjs) is directly reusable across three strands without new design
mixed-scope splitting required at item level not category level -- memory files, settings.local.json, git config all internally mixed
no dotfiles manager has solved ephemeral-host detection natively -- JASON-OS needs ~2 lines of code for v1
serendipitous_findings: config.local.toml contains a confirmed live API key -- canonical example for machine_exclude at registration
PR #10 R2 (776 path substitutions) is accidental prior art for the content-time detection approach
preview.js rollback pattern maps directly to ledger-last rollback -- simpler than expected
memory type enum (enums.json) provides a natural scope-classification lookup table -- only needs the mapping defined once
No dotfile manager provides automated heuristic machine-bound detection -- Layer 2 (content-time) approach is novel
~/.claude/projects/ directory names are reversible path encodings not hashes -- enables correct multi-machine sync logic
top_open_questions_for_planning: unit_type and verdict enum final values (context-artifact vs memory vs config splits; observe-only for extract)
settings.local.json sub-file sync granularity (sidecar file pattern recommended by gap pursuit -- planning must ratify)
CLAUDE.md tweak block identification convention (sentinel comment or section-header naming not yet defined)
tenet identification mechanism (filename prefix recommended for v1 -- planning must confirm)
memory_type to scope-tag mapping table (natural classification mechanism, just needs the mapping defined once)
ledger.jsonl as context-sync category 19 (planning decision: should ledger travel across machines?)
ephemeral-machine guard implementation location (skill vs shared utility)
calibration_parameters_estimated: 0.8 fast-path suppression confidence threshold (no empirical basis; implement as named constant)
>60% cache hit rate aspirational target (no empirical basis pre-implementation)
sharding threshold 2600-4000+ records (derived from measurement; ESTIMATED range not hard constant)
synthesis_notes: Version 2.0: post-gap-pursuit final synthesis. Core architecture remains HIGH confidence. 4 refuted sub-claims corrected. 5 disputes resolved with explicit planning actions. 18 gap-pursuit claims added. Key concession: profile_slice_hash hashes full profile at v1 (slice optimization post-recipe-library). Ephemeral-machine guard added as v1 requirement. Sidecar file pattern recommended for settings.local.json sub-file granularity.
